heroPoems.title: Mother o' Mine
heroPoems.firstStanza:
If I were hanged on the highest hill,
_Mother o' mine, O mother o' mine!_
I know whose love would follow me still,
_Mother o' mine, O mother o' mine!_

heroPoems.title: Meditation as Repeated Return
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Prepare the body.
Regulate habits.
Simplify environment.
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Counsel in the Heart
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Anxiety in a person's heart weighs it down,
but a good word makes it glad.
The righteous choose friends carefully,
but the way of the wicked leads astray.

heroPoems.title: Entrance
The Quiet Power
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Know others,
and you are perceptive.
Know yourself,
and you are clear.
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: Wild roses from the field
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Wild roses from the field,
frogs calling everywhere.
Float the petals in wine.
Enjoy this brief hour.

heroPoems.title: Leave this chanting and singing and telling of
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Leave this chanting and singing and telling of beads! Whom dost thou
worship in this lonely dark corner of a temple with doors all shut?
Open thine eyes and see thy God is not before thee!
He is there where the tiller is tilling the hard ground and where the
pathmaker is breaking stones. He is with them in sun and in shower, and
his garment is covered with dust. Put of thy holy mantle and even like
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Aunt Helen
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Miss Helen Slingsby was my maiden aunt,
And lived in a small house near a fashionable square
Cared for by servants to the number of four.
Now when she died there was silence in heaven
And silence at her end of the street.
The shutters were drawn and the undertaker wiped his feet--

heroPoems.title: The Runaway
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Once when the snow of the year was beginning to fall,
We stopped by a mountain pasture to say, “Whose colt?”
A little Morgan had one forefoot on the wall,
The other curled at his breast. He dipped his head
And snorted at us. And then he had to bolt.
We heard the miniature thunder where he fled,
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: On Love
heroPoems.firstStanza: And he raised his head and looked upon the people, and there fell a stillness upon them. And with a great voice he said:

collections.name: The Drift of Old Keys
collections.tagline: For slow hours with open books.
collections.theme: Poems that trace the distance between nearness and speech.

featuredPoem.title: From Hearing to Decision
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Instruction was given.
Doubt was examined.

collections.name: A Margin for Breathing
collections.tagline: For when light falls in stripes.
collections.theme: An atmosphere of low light, small rituals, and unhurried noticing.

featuredPoem.title: Whenever Justice Fades
featuredPoem.firstLines:
When truth thins,
when cruelty rises,

collections.name: The Quiet That Carries
collections.tagline: For when small sounds matter most.
collections.theme: A compact emotional landscape of return, pause, and half-spoken thought.

featuredPoem.title: A Bundle of Myrrh
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth—
your love is better than wine.

poems.title: I Am Much Too Alone in This World
